title: Senior Business Development Executive / Business Development Executive
description:
About the Role

At Setia Law LLC, we pride ourselves on being a dynamic and forward‑thinking law firm with a strong reputation for excellence and client service.

We are seeking a motivated and accomplished Business Development Executive / Senior Executive to join our growing and highly regarded team.

This is an exciting opportunity for a business development professional who wants to make a real impact in a leading law firm environment.

You will work closely with our lawyers and senior stakeholders to drive strategic initiatives, support high‑profile client programmes, and contribute to the firm’s growth story.

As part of Setia Law, you will:

Drive client development by preparing winning pitches, supporting account planning, and strengthening key client relationships.       
Build market intelligence through research on clients, competitors, and sectors, positioning the firm to capture new opportunities. 
Enhance the firm’s profile by coordinating conferences, sponsorships, industry directory submissions, and maintaining credentials such as awards and rankings.  
Shape digital communications by advising on the effective use of social media, e‑marketing, client alerts, and the firm’s knowledge portal.
Accelerate your career growth, expand your professional network, and gain valuable exposure to high‑value clients, international platforms, and strategic business development initiatives.

Key Responsibilities (Detailed Breakdown)

1. Client Development

  • Coordinate the preparation of client RFPs, pitches, presentations, and Gebiz tenders.
  • Organise and manage regular key client team meetings to ensure they are productive, action‑oriented, and aligned with agreed client strategies.
  • Assist key client team in developing and implementing account plans to pursue strategic priorities, meet financial targets, and strengthen client relationships.
  • Support business development events, including guest list management, invitations, venuecoordination, materials preparation, and on‑site logistics.

2. Market Intelligence

  • Conduct research on existing and prospective clients, competitor activity, and market trends to identify opportunities for growth and expansion.
  • Build a strong understanding of priority sectors, deal activity, and market developments to support strategic targeting and client acquisition.

3. Profile Building & Communications

  • Coordinate the firm’s participation in sponsorships, conferences (e.g., INSOL), memberships, and business development trips.
  • Prepare/Manage submissions and coordinate interviews for industry publications such as Chambers Asia Pacific, IFLR1000, and Legal 500 Asia Pacific.
  • Draft new collateral content such as capability statements, brochures, legal profiles, and manage contributions of Asia-focused material to international marketing resources.
  • Advise on the effective use of digital and social media to raise the practice profile, including management of the firm’s social media accounts, website, client knowledge portal, e‑marketing, client alerts, bulletins, and client updates.

4. Knowledge Management

  • Collect and track representative matters, maintain the firm’s experience database, and collate credentials such as awards, rankings, and accolades.

5. Events, seminars and conferences

  • Project manage client events including active participation in them, e.g. identifying the right people internally to attend/introduce

What We’re Looking For

To succeed in this role, you should bring a mix of professional experience, practical skills, and the right mindset:

Experience

  • At least 5 years of experience in business development or marketing (professional services industry experience preferred).
  • Tertiary qualifications in Marketing, Business, Law, or a related discipline.
  • Familiarity with CRM systems is an advantage.

Skills

  • Strong proficiency in both written and spoken English. Ability to communicate in Chinese will be viewed favourably.
  • Excellent project management skills with keen attention to detail.
  • Confident in working collaboratively with individuals at all levels of the organisation.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint).
  • Strong business acumen and analytical skills.

Mindset

  • High level of professionalism, integrity, and commitment to excellence.
  • Flexible and adaptable, able to thrive in a fast‑paced environment.
  • Proactive and driven, with the ability to take initiative and see projects through independently.

postedCompany.description:
Setia Law LLC (SETIA) is a specialist high-stakes dispute resolution and crisis management team in Singapore, built on formidable experience, driven by ingenuity, and rooted in integrity.


SETIA is helmed by deeply experienced lawyers who have been at the forefront of major global crises and are specialists in Dispute Resolution, Fraud and Financial Crimes, Restructuring & Insolvency, and International Enforcement.


SETIA draws on its experience and knowledge to deliver expert legal advice and representation to clients in high-stakes commercial dispute and crisis situations, whilst remaining rooted to its core values of respect, resilience and reliability.
postedCompany.name: SETIA LAW LLC

districts.location: D01 Cecil, Marina, People’s Park, Raffles Place
